prose:
The frontier orbital energies for pyridine were computed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier `standard`) with the PySCF program. The calculation was performed in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was sourced from the provided input file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`, and was not optimized in this step (single-point calculation). The SCF converged in 10 cycles. No warnings were reported.

The Highest Occupied Molecular Orbital (HOMO) energy is -7.2526 eV, and the Lowest Unoccupied Molecular Orbital (LUMO) energy is -1.1128 eV. The resulting HOMO-LUMO gap is 6.1398 eV.
